page.content: The table presents various genes along with their respective scores, descriptions, supporting evidence, and PubMed IDs. The gene HAND2 scores 6.87 and is described as a heart- and neural crest derivatives-expressed protein 2, essential for cardiac morphogenesis, particularly for the formation of the right ventricle and the aortic arch arteries, supported by a population study citing various mutations found in TOF patients, with PubMed ID 20819618. FOS, with a score of 4.09, is a nuclear phosphoprotein interacting with JUN/AP-1 transcription factor, critically regulating the development of cells destined to maintain the skeleton, as per literature text-mining indicating cardiac hypertrophy, supported by PubMed IDs 16696897, 10328763, 12713689, and 16259952. NOTCH2, scoring 2.5, functions as a receptor for membrane-bound ligands Jagged1, Jagged2, and Delta1 to regulate cell-fate determination, with supporting evidence from a population study of various mutations found in CHD patients, noted under PubMed ID 16773578.

found in CHD patients, noted under PubMed ID 16773578.

page.content: MLLT4 scores 1.72 and is part of an adhesion system involved in cell-cell adherens junctions, with supporting evidence paralleling functions to FLNA and GJA1, citing references. THBS1 scores 1.7, mediating cell-to-cell and cell-to-matrix interactions, with genetic associations related to myocardial infarct, supported by PubMed IDs 12482844 and 16684956. MAPK14, scoring 1.69, integrates multiple biochemical signals affecting various cellular processes, with literature text-mining supporting pulmonary disease chronic obstructive, cited under PubMed IDs 19880675, 17959643, 20093202, and 19004925. ELK1, scoring 1.56, forms a ternary complex with serum response factors, with supporting evidence referenced. MAPKAPK5 scores 1.54, similar to MAPK14. DCN, with a score of 1.52, is a connective tissue component binding type I collagen fibrils, supported by literature text-mining related to myocardial infarction, heart failure, congenital malformation, and vascular diseases, noted under PubMed IDs 17558846, 9162605, 18514055, and 9493904. Finally, NUMB scores 1.49, playing a role in cell fate determination during development, associated with disease gene NOTCH1, with supporting evidence from the MGI database regarding targeted knock-out in mice affecting cardiovascular systems, cited under PubMed ID 11412999.
